2606.11901.title: DuoBench: A Reproducible Benchmark for Bimanual Manipulation in Simulation and the Real World
2606.11901.paper_summary: Bimanual robot systems substantially expand manipulation capabilities, but coordinating two arms introduces additional control complexity and failure modes that are not well captured by existing benchmarks. We introduce DuoBench, an extensible benchmarking framework for bimanual manipulation policies on the FR3 Duo platform. DuoBench comprises eleven tasks spanning four coordination categories, implemented in simulation and partially reproduced in the real world through reproducible task recipes with 3D-printable assets. In addition, we propose a stage-based evaluation scheme that supports fine-grained semantic failure analysis beyond binary success and provide human-teleoperated datasets for all benchmark tasks. We benchmark several dual-arm imitation-learning and vision-language-action policies in simulation and on real hardware. Our results show that current policies remain challenged by bimanual manipulation, particularly in early interaction stages, parallel arm execution, and transfer between simulation and real-world settings. DuoBench provides a reproducible testbed for diagnosing these failure modes and studying future methods for dual-arm policy learning. Code, datasets, and videos are available at https://duobench.github.io/
2606.11901.paper_summary_zh: 雙臂機器人系統大幅擴展了操作能力，但協調兩個手臂引入了額外的控制複雜性和失效模式，而這些在現有基準中並未得到很好的捕捉。我們推出了DuoBench，一個可擴展的基準框架，用於FR3 Duo平台上的雙臂操作策略。DuoBench包含十一個任務，涵蓋四個協調類別，這些任務在模擬中實現，並通過可重現的任務配方和3D可打印資產在現實世界中部分重現。此外，我們提出了一種基於階段的評估方案，支持細粒度的語義失效分析，超越二元成功，並為所有基準任務提供人類遠程操作的數據集。我們在模擬和實際硬體上基準測試了幾種雙臂模仿學習和視覺-語言-行動策略。我們的結果顯示，當前的策略在雙臂操作中仍然面臨挑戰，特別是在早期交互階段、平行手臂執行以及模擬與現實世界環境之間的轉換。DuoBench提供了一個可重現的測試平台，用於診斷這些失效模式並研究未來的雙臂策略學習方法。代碼、數據集和視頻可在 https://duobench.github.io/ 獲得。
2606.11901.author: Tobias Jülg et.al.
2606.11901.authors: Tobias Jülg, Seongjin Bien, Simon Hilber, Yannik Blei, Pierre Krack, Maximilian Li, Sven Parusel, Rudolf Lioutikov, Florian Walter, Wolfram Burgard
